Third Amended Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16
Off Calendar Jun-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WUWTA, INC's Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Dr. Mehdi Matin and M. Matin Surgical and Dental Group, Inc. succeeded in part on a special motion to strike Plaintiff WUWTA, Inc.'s complaint pursuant to CCP 425.16. They now seek fees and costs in the amount of $46,772.10. The court grants this unopposed motion.

An award of fees and costs is mandatory to a prevailing defendant on an anti-SLAPP motion. (CCP 425.16 (c)(1).) "[A] defendant who brings a successful special motion to strike is entitled . . . to reasonable attorney fees . . . ." (Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 491.) "The amount of an attorney fee award under the anti-SLAPP statute is computed by the trial court in accordance with the familiar lodestar method. Under that method, the court tabulates the attorney fee touchstone, or lodestar, by multiplying the number of hours reasonably expended by the reasonable hourly rate prevailing in the community for similar work." (Id. [quotation marks and citations omitted].) The court has reviewed the billing records and requested rates provided in the motion. Both hours billed and hourly rates are reasonable and supported in the moving papers. The court therefore awards fees of $46,772.10 to these defendants.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MILES WOODLIEF, Esq. of Archer (tel: 415-730-3032) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JON JAMES, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley and Feeney (tel: 415-788-1900) for Defendants Dr. Mehdi Matin and M. Matin Surgical And Dental Group, Inc.
Atty. BRETT RUBIN, Esq. of the Rubin Law Firm (tel: 310-923-6758) specially appearing for Defendant Mina Katchooi.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS DR. MEHDI MATIN, AND M. MATIN SUGICAL AND DENTAL GROUP INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. Counsel Brett Rubin makes oral request for joinder. Counsel Miles Woodlief objects to Defendant Mina Katchooi joining the motion to strike thereof. The Court denies Counsel Brett Rubin request for joinder. Matter argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

This case arises from a contract between M. Matin Surgical and Dental Group ("Matin Group") and WUWTA, Inc. for marketing services. Defendant Mehdi Matin is the principal of Matin Group. Matin and Matin Group have filed this special motion to strike the complaint as a SLAPP. (CCP 425.16.) Defendant Mina Katchooi is not a party to the motion (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
